name: Barbarito Diez
description: '''Barbarito Díez''' (December 4, 1910 – May 6, 1995) was a Cuban singer who specialized in [[danzón]]. He began his career as the singer for Graciano Gómez and [[Isaac Oviedo]]'s [[son cubano|son]] group, before joining [[Antonio María Romeu]]'s orchestra. As the lead vocalist for Romeu's ensemble for 20 years, he established himself as one of the main exponents of the sung danzón. He continued singing with his own [[Charanga (Cuba)|charanga]], as well as other groups, for another 30 years. He also toured and recorded in [[Venezuela]] and [[Puerto Rico]] before retiring in the early 1990s, when complications from [[Diabetes mellitus|diabetes]] prevented him from performing and eventually resulted in his death in 1995. A naturally-gifted [[tenor]], he was known for his sense of rhythm, correct diction and romantic style.
